introspection: Strong recovery day. Balance surged +$14.04 to $100.48 — best single-day P&L this week. Three settlements closed (2 wins confirmed, 1 pending), SPHI_DECAY harvests flipped from Sunday's -$5.59 drag to a +$8.90 contributor. The concern: phantom position warnings escalated to 39 (up 77% from Sunday's 22). The local/API state drift is compounding and will degrade execution quality if not fixed this week. Week-to-date: +$10.23 (+11.3%). I'm trading well — the infrastructure just needs to keep up.
research: Focused on three areas today: (1) Phantom position root cause analysis — compared 39 warnings against harvest timing and found strong correlation; each failed harvest sync creates a new phantom. (2) SPHI_DECAY behavior analysis — Sunday it was a drag, Monday a strong contributor. Hypothesis: post-weekend volume recovery improves harvest exit quality. Need 2-3 more data points to confirm. (3) Position sizing via Kelly Criterion — still modeling against historical 542 trades. Initial results suggest current sizing is conservative by ~30%.
changes: Three days of missed analysis now produced (Sat-Mon). Analysis files written to agents/aether/analysis/. All three published to HQ feed as aether-analysis report type. Week-to-date summary table added to Monday's report. Identified kai_analysis.py scheduling gap as root cause of missed analysis — filed for launchd wiring.
followUps: P0: Fix phantom position sync — overwrite local state with API state at cycle start
P1: Wire kai_analysis.py into launchd (daily 23:00 MT) so analysis generates automatically
P2: Add per-harvest P&L tracking to eliminate balance reconciliation gaps

forKai: Three days of analysis backfilled and published. Week-to-date: +$10.23 (+11.3%), which is solid. The phantom position issue is the single biggest risk — 39 warnings Monday and climbing. Without a fix, execution quality will degrade by end of week. Also: kai_analysis.py has no scheduled trigger, which is why these analyses were missed in the first place. Please wire it into launchd.
